description_long: This is a small, rustic house with a steeply pitched, thatched roof that extends well beyond the walls, providing ample coverage and a traditional appearance. The roof is constructed from thick bundles of straw or reeds, with a wooden framework visible at the peak, topped by a simple finial. The walls are made of dark, woven material, likely wood or reeds, giving the structure a hand-crafted, organic look. The base of the house is surrounded by a wooden platform or porch, with uneven planks and some visible wear, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. There are no visible windows, and the entryway is a simple opening with a few wooden planks leaning against it, possibly serving as a makeshift door. The overall design is reminiscent of vernacular rural dwellings found in various traditional societies.
description: A small, rustic house with a steep thatched roof, dark woven walls, and a wooden platform base. The structure has a hand-crafted, traditional appearance with no visible windows and a simple entryway.
description_view_0: The house is seen from a high corner angle, highlighting the thick, conical thatched roof and the wooden framework at the peak.
description_view_1: This view shows the mossy, weathered side of the roof and the main entryway, with wooden planks leaning against the porch.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the overhanging thatched roof and the uneven wooden platform, with the entryway and planks more visible.
description_view_3: The rear corner view displays the uninterrupted thatched roof and the continuous wooden platform, with no entryway visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple open entryway with leaning wooden planks, no steps or porch roof, rustic and utilitarian
